highlights.text: My general lean is bulls need to hold 6843 ideally.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
There are a few of these cycles, but the foundation of them is what I call the two siblings: Elevator down (sibling 1) , into Failed Breakdown, into short squeeze (sibling 2) .
Elevator Down refers to vertical red candles where no support matters.
These short squeezes are caused by one thing: My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
In the morning we went elevator down 70+ points down to 6791.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “If this is the case, the obvious entry is to wait for 6795 to defend (doesn’t need to be exact) then recover today’s 6808 daily low.” Tuesday morning ES flushed down to 6791, recovered 6808, and we ripped hard to 6860+.
I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “In summary for tomorrow: ES put in a big Failed Breakdown today by trapping under Friday’s 6808 low down to 6791, and recovering 6808.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.
Targets are 6884, dip, then 6898, 6913, 6925-30, then 6954 which is a big magnet.” ES again wasted no time on this, getting to 6925-30 by 10:05AM.
In doing so, ES lost Friday’s daily low at 6808.

post_title: Bulls Win The Week In SPX. Can They Seal The Deal Though? Feb 23 Plan
